message:
feat(runtime): add join_with_drop_reason for explicit abort attribution

When race combinators drop losing futures, the abort reason should be
explicitly "race loser" rather than the generic "dropped" reason. This
improves debugging and allows distinguishing intentional cancellation
from unexpected drops.

Changes:
- Add TaskHandle::join_with_drop_reason() method that stores the reason
  to use when the JoinFuture is dropped before completion
- Update JoinFuture to track optional drop_reason
- Modify JoinFuture::drop to use the stored reason if present
- Update race/race_all in Scope to use join_with_drop_reason with
  CancelReason::race_loser()
- Add clarifying comments about double-abort being idempotent

This makes the cancellation attribution explicit: when a task loses a
race, logs and error handling see "race_loser" instead of just "dropped",
making it clearer what happened.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
